channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel says cease-fire begins after 3-hour delay over list of hostage names
item.description: A cease-fire went into effect Sunday after Israel confirmed it had received the first three names of the hostages who are set to be released. The cease-fire was delayed for three hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's impeached president is arrested over martial law declaration and his supporters riot
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hours after South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was formally arrested, triggering rioting by his supporters, his lawyers said Sunday that he remains defiant in his refusal to answer questions over the probe into his declaration of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US rejects Venezuelan President Maduro's reelection, but keeps financial lifeline for his government
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Things seemed to be looking up for Venezuela in 2022. Following years of authoritarian rule and withering economic sanctions, President Nicolás Maduro had agreed to work toward a democratic presidential election.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An imprisoned Nobel laureate underscores human rights abuses in Belarus
item.description: Ales Bialiatski, a human rights advocate who won the 2022 Nobel Peace Prize, is serving a 10-year prison sentence in Belarus and is one of hundreds of its political prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's impeached president is arrested over a martial law declaration as his supporters riot
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was formally arrested early on Sunday, days after being apprehended at his presidential compound in Seoul. He faces possible imprisonment over his ill-fated declaration of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to expect as Israel-Hamas cease-fire goes into effect on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli government approved the deal early Saturday morning for a cease-fire in Gaza and the release of hostages captured after Hamas' terrorist attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu praises Trump in 'temporary' ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu praised President-elect Trump for his role in negotiating a ceasefire between the nation and the militant group Hamas. “From the moment he was elected, President Trump has been involved in the mission for the release of our hostages.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Journalists in Haiti demand justice as they bury a second colleague killed by gangs
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The young boy wore a suit and bow tie to his stepfather’s funeral on Saturday, where he demanded a handkerchief and wiped tears off his mother’s face as they buried Marckendy Natoux, one of two journalists killed by gangs in Haiti.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exploring Greenland's majestic history as President-elect Trump shines spotlight on island nation
item.description: Greenland is the world's largest island, but most of it is covered by ice. Its southern tip, however, is sprinkled with picturesque towns and villages that are isolated from each other.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 Supreme Court judges in Iran's capital before turning gun on himself, state media says
item.description: Two Iranian Supreme Court judges were shot and killed in Tehran on Saturday while a third judge and a bodyguard were wounded, although the motive is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vowed to end the war in Ukraine quickly but Moscow and Kyiv are digging in before any talks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has pledged to broker a peace deal in Ukraine, but as he prepares to take office, peace seems as elusive as ever. Moscow and Kyiv are seeking battlefield gains to strengthen their negotiating positions ahead of any prospective talks to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — More than 40 Pakistanis were feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a boat off West Africa's Atlantic coastline, which has emerged as a primary point of departure for migrants aiming to reach Europe.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN announces new plan to counter the surge in antisemitism
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations announced new actions Friday to counter the surge in antisemitism, including encouraging governments to enforce laws against hate crimes and discrimination. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about the hostages and cease-fire deal between Israel and Hamas set to begin Sunday
item.description: Israel and Hamas finalize a historic cease-fire and hostage deal set to begin Sunday. The agreement includes phased releases, intense negotiations and ongoing security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daughter of one of the oldest Israeli hostages hopes for answers in ceasefire deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sharone Lifschitz is well aware that the odds are against her 84-year-old father. As one of the oldest hostages taken by Hamas, Oded Lifshitz would be among the first to be released under a ceasefire deal expected to begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuania vows to boost defense spending to 5-6% of GDP, citing the threat of Russian aggression
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuania has decided to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of overall national economic output starting in 2026 due to the threat of Russian aggression in the region, Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British PM Starmer visits Auschwitz, vows to fight antisemitism ahead of security talks in Poland
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday visited the Nazi German extermination camp at Auschwitz, voicing his “sheer horror” at what he saw there, before holding talks with Poland's leaders on stepping up European defense and tightening Britain's ties with the European Union.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-AIDS program in GOP crosshairs over abortion funding
item.description: Republicans are fuming at the State Department after learning that recipients of funding from the legacy program to curb HIV/AIDS across Africa, PEPFAR, performed abortions in violation of U.S. law. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Xi discuss TikTok, trade in call before inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump said Friday he spoke with Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders discussing trade and the fate of the popular video-sharing app TikTok. "The call was a very good one for both China and the U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Russia and Iran have a troubled history despite their current alliance
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin hosted his Iranian counterpart Friday for the signing of a broad pact between Moscow and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaza is in ruins, and it's unclear how it will be rebuilt
item.description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are eager to leave miserable tent camps and return to their homes if a long-awaited ceasefire agreement halts the Israel-Hamas war, but many will find there is nothing left and no way to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Starmer signs ‘100-year partnership’ agreement with Ukraine during trip to Kyiv
item.description: As part of a European show of support, U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has signed a 100-year partnership agreement with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis injured as Vatican confirms 2nd fall in matter of weeks
item.description: Pope Francis fell for the second time in six weeks on Thursday, injuring his right arm and requiring a sling, the Vatican says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tsunami alert lifted after magnitude 6.6 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan issued a tsunami advisory on Monday after a magnitude 6.6 earthquake struck the southwest of the country. The public warnings to stay away from coastal areas were later lifted. The agency initially gave the magnitude estimate at 6.9, but later revised it down.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Baby gorilla finds home at Istanbul zoo after rescue
item.description: Five-month-old Zeytin is recovering at an Istanbul zoo after being rescued from a cargo hold on a Turkish Airlines flight.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
